Donation-Recipient Classification: A taxonomy of where charitable dollars land, with payout/transparency traits and where Earthbux fits.

Unnamed: 2: Endowment from one donor/family, governed independently of donor's company; makes grants to other charities.
Unnamed: 5: High - grants itemized on public Form 990-PF

Unnamed: 2: Private foundation governed substantially by the donor family.
Funded by a company; grants tied to its industry/communities.
Unnamed: 6: Wells Fargo Fdn, Novo Nordisk Fdn

Unnamed: 2: Private foundation that runs its OWN programs rather than granting out.
Public charity pooling many donors' gifts for a geographic area; hosts DAFs and runs local grants.
Unnamed: 5: Medium - files 990; DAF accounts not itemized
Unnamed: 6: Silicon Valley CF, Chicago Community Trust

Unnamed: 2: For-profit-affiliated charity holding donor-advised accounts; donor recommends grants over time.
Unnamed: 6: Fidelity Charitable, NPT, DAFgiving360, Vanguard Charitable

Unnamed: 2: Pools gifts around one cause or umbrella, regrants to member orgs.
Unnamed: 6: United Way, Global Fund, Jewish federations

Unnamed: 2: Members pool money and collectively decide grants; often small, democratic.
Unnamed: 5: Varies - often very open to members
Unnamed: 6: Philanthropy Together circles, mutual aid networks

Unnamed: 2: Delivers services itself (relief, research, education); the end recipient.
University/hospital holding a permanent investment fund for the institution.
Split-interest trust giving income to charity or donor for a term, remainder to the other.
Tech intermediary routing many small gifts to vetted nonprofits.
Public-money grantmaker (not a donation recipient, but a peer funder).

Unnamed: 1: Democratic pooled giving + independent media oversight = a transparent, fast-payout hybrid of a giving circle and a community foundation, with a watchdog newsroom (Earthbux News) attached.
Unnamed: 4: Pays out per mission cycle (weeks, not years)
Unnamed: 5: HIGH by design - public votes, public budgets, parallel reporting
